title: hieracium murorum subsp. fennoorbicans
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. fennoorbicans, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of plant in the dandelion family that is native to Finland and other parts of Northern Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The health benefits of Wall Hawkweed include its ability to support digestion, boost the immune system, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
